channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa requires R250 billion over ten years for minimal climate adaptation measures
item.description: South Africa needs a total of R250 billion, which is about US$15.64 billion, spread over the next ten years just to reach the basic level of adapting to climate change effects. Researchers from the Un...

item.title: Robert Mugabe’s Son Faces Attempted Murder and Justice Obstruction Charges in Johannesburg
item.description: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, the youngest son of late Zimbabwean president Robert Mugabe, appeared again before the Johannesburg court on Tuesday, alongside co-accused Tobias Matonhodze. The two men fa...

item.title: Chancellor Reeves outlines targeted support for rising energy costs amid ongoing conflict
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves has indicated that the UK government is preparing measures to assist those most affected by rising energy bills, which could surge due to the ongoing US-Israel conflict with I...

item.title: Population in Atlantic Canada’s major cities grows above national average despite slower immigration
item.description: Atlantic Canada’s major cities experienced population growth exceeding the national average between July 2024 and July 2025, according to a report by the Atlantic Economic Council. While international...

item.title: Recommended 2026 K-dramas available for streaming
item.description: This article from ABS-CBN News encourages viewers who prefer staying indoors to explore a selection of upcoming South Korean dramas set for release or streaming in 2026. It highlights various K-dramas...

item.title: Crunchyroll Investigates Data Breach Involving Customer Support Ticket System
item.description: Crunchyroll, the popular anime streaming platform, has confirmed a data breach that occurred after a hacker claimed to have gained unauthorized access to its internal systems and customer data. The in...

item.title: The Canadian Film Fest Celebrates 20th Anniversary with ‘Eyeballs Up’ Campaign
item.description: The Canadian Film Fest (CFF) is celebrating its 20th anniversary in 2026, marking a significant evolution from its humble beginnings. Founded by Bern Euler in his condo, where he initially showcased C...

item.title: Iran-Israel Conflict Driving Up Edible Oil Prices in India
item.description: The ongoing war between Iran and Israel has begun to affect the Indian consumer market, particularly in the kitchen. As a result of the conflict, prices of edible oils such as sunflower and palm oil h...

item.title: Tezgam Express Derails Near Bahawalpur, Rescue Operations Underway
item.description: On Tuesday night, the Karachi-bound Tezgam Express met with a serious incident as it derailed near Bahawalpur in Punjab. Exact details about how the derailment happened are not yet clear, and authorit...

item.title: Tanzania and South Africa strengthen Kiswahili teaching and education cooperation
item.description: Tanzania and South Africa are taking stronger steps to put into action the 2022 Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation in basic education, especially focusing on teaching Kiswahili in South Africa...

item.title: Finance Ministry Confirms Petrol Cargoes for March and April Largely Secured Amid Global Oil Concerns
item.description: The Finance Ministry of Pakistan has assured that petrol cargoes for the current month of March and the upcoming month of April have largely been secured. This information was shared during a meeting ...

item.title: Three Teenagers Arrested in Connection with Alleged Canberra Drug Syndicate
item.description: Three teenagers, aged between 18 and 19, have been arrested in Queanbeyan and Canberra in connection to a suspected dial-a-dealer drug ring. On March 20, police stopped two 19-year-olds in a car where...

item.title: Australia's Conservation Efforts Need Strategic Focus to Protect Endangered Species
item.description: Australia has made significant strides in conservation, protecting around 22% of its land and aiming for 30% by 2030. However, recent analysis shows that much of this protected land is in remote or le...

item.title: Fuel Price Increases Set to Revive Debate on Remote and Hybrid Work Models
item.description: With the expected fuel price hikes in April, set to reach R5/litre for petrol, the focus on working from home (WFH) and hybrid work models is likely to intensify. Independent economist John Loos warns...

item.title: ICRC and Nigerian Red Cross Deny False Claims of Aiding Insurgents in North-East Nigeria
item.description: The International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) and the Nigerian Red Cross Society (NRCS) don release joint statement on Tuesday wey dem debunk social media allegations say dem dey support armed g...

item.title: Australia-EU Trade Deal: Winners, Losers, and Compromises
item.description: After eight years of negotiations, Australia and the European Union have signed a free trade agreement in Canberra. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and EU President Ursula von der Leyen hailed the dea...

item.title: MK Party MP and Municipal Official Arrested for R1.6 Million Water Tender Fraud
item.description: The Hawks have apprehended MK Party MP Mandlenkosi James Matutu and Joe Gqabi District Municipality water supply manager Thembelani Ngceba in connection with a fraudulent water infrastructure tender. ...

item.title: Niger Delta Leaders Urge Indigenes to Invest and Close Economic Gap with South West
item.description: The Nigeria Delta Chamber of Commerce, Trade, Mines and Agriculture (NDCCTMA) and the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) have called on entrepreneurs from the Niger Delta to invest in the regio...

item.title: Farmers file complaint against Harry Roque over disputed agrarian land transfer
item.description: A group of around 20 farmers from Mariveles, Bataan has formally filed a land grabbing complaint against former presidential spokesman Harry Roque and several others. The case involves land parcels un...

item.title: Riz Ahmed Explores Race and Identity in His New Comedy Series Bait
item.description: Riz Ahmed's new comedy series, Bait, centres on Shah Latif, a British-Asian actor who finds himself considered for the role of James Bond. The show cleverly examines issues of racial representation, B...
